stimulus: With its sweeping limestone façade and towering twin spires, the National Cathedral in Washington, D.C., is among the most recognizable buildings in the United States. However, many people do not realize that ______blank built over the course of eighty-three years, with construction beginning in 1907 and ending in 1990.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is pronoun-antecedent agreement. The antecedent is the singular noun phrase "the National Cathedral," so the singular pronoun "it" (with the singular verb "was") is required.

Choice B is incorrect because "one" doesn't clearly refer back to the National Cathedral. Choice C is incorrect because the plural "they were" doesn't agree with the singular antecedent. Choice D is incorrect because "both" implies two referents, but only one building is mentioned.
